features.description: First-time non-technical founder running a 5-person seed-stage company. Pain: cannot afford full-time growth marketer. Channels: LinkedIn, Twitter/X, founder Slack groups. Pickup vocabulary: 'GTM', 'product-led', 'distribution'. Will pay for tools that compress a week of work into a day. Brand wants the CTA to land on the free-tier signup.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 variants name a concrete time-saving outcome.
At least one variant uses founder-vocabulary (GTM, distribution, product-led).
CTA in every variant points to the free-tier signup.
review_criteria: No fake testimonials or invented stats.
No urgency-led variants that imply a fake deadline.

features.description: Senior engineer at a 50–500 person company evaluating a tool to recommend internally. Pain: needs to be able to defend the choice to a skeptical CTO. Channels: HN, technical newsletters, GitHub trending. Pickup vocabulary: 'self-host', 'open source', 'no vendor lock-in'. Brand wants the CTA to land on the docs index.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 variants name a concrete technical differentiator.
At least one variant uses 'self-host' or 'open source' explicitly.
CTA in every variant points to the docs index.
review_criteria: No claims about scale or performance without a number.
No emoji-heavy variants (engineer-evaluator audience reads them as cringe).

features.description: Engineering team lead at a 10-person team rolling the tool out to their reports. Pain: needs onboarding to be 30 minutes max. Channels: engineering-leadership newsletters, lead-engineer Slack communities. Pickup vocabulary: 'onboarding', 'team adoption', 'rollout'. Brand wants the CTA to land on the team-tier pricing page.
acceptance_criteria: All 5 variants name a concrete team-rollout outcome.
At least one variant addresses the 30-minute onboarding pain directly.
CTA in every variant points to the team-tier pricing page.
review_criteria: No assumptions about team size beyond '10-person team'.
No variants that overpromise free seats.
